role: This file is the visual rendering component of the wheel instrument, drawing a responsive SVG structure grid with selectable data points.
capabilities: Responsive SVG sizing via useSize hook
Render dyadic nested grid lines by recursion depth
Render square outline and concentric inscribed circles
Render central axes and axis/circle coincidence points
Render animated data points colored by sector with selection highlight
Render transparent hit targets for point selection
Emit onPick callback when a point is clicked
Apply motion feel transitions to point enter/move/exit
substance_why: The file fully implements geometry layout, decorative grid/circle/axis drawing, animated point rendering, selection feedback, and pointer hit handling with no TODOs or stubs visible.
interactions_present: Click a point's invisible hit circle to invoke onPick
Visual selection feedback via larger radius and primary stroke
interactions_missing: Focus management and ARIA live selection announcements
performance_risks: AnimatePresence + layoutId over all proj.points can cause layout thrashing with many points
Dyadic grid line count grows exponentially with proj.grid
place(), levels, and sectorIndex are recomputed on every render for every point and line
Duplicate iteration over proj.points for animated dots and hit targets doubles work
Framer-motion layout animations on every point may jank during resize or data updates
reuse_verdict_why: The geometric concepts and visual language are domain-specific and worth keeping, but the rendering, animation, and hit-layer should be rebuilt with cleaner separation and performance safeguards.
decomposition_hints: Extract pure geometry helpers (place, dyadicLevels, ringR, coincidence points) into a wheel/math module or hook
Split static structure rendering (grid, circles, axes, box) into a separate StructureLayer component
Split animated point layer and hit layer into PointLayer and HitLayer components
Make animation strategy pluggable or memoized to avoid re-mounting all points on every update
